enrichment.short_description: SeedDMS is a free, open-source, self-hosted document management system built in PHP that helps small and mid-sized organizations store, version, and control access to documents with configurable workflows.
enrichment.company_size: Community-maintained open-source project, not a company; developed primarily by a single lead maintainer with volunteer contributors
enrichment.seo_title: SeedDMS Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SeedDMS is a free, open-source document management system for self-hosting. See its features, requirements, pricing (it is free), and how it compares to paid DMS tools.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SeedDMS is a free and open-source document management system (DMS) written in PHP, built for organizations that want to self-host document storage, versioning, and workflow control without paying software licensing fees. The project traces its lineage to myDMS, which began development around 2002 and was later renamed LetoDMS around 2008; in February 2013, lead developer Uwe Steinmann forked the codebase and renamed it SeedDMS after a dispute over the direction of the original project, and he has remained its primary maintainer ever since, with the project now in its second decade of active development. SeedDMS runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows, and supports MySQL, SQLite3, or PostgreSQL as its database backend, so it can be deployed on shared hosting, a private server, or a home lab with no per-user or per-document licensing limits. Its core feature set includes a fully web-based interface, storage of every document version with rollback, granular user and group permissions, external authentication support, configurable email notifications on document changes, and a workflow engine for review, approval, and revision cycles. SeedDMS also supports fulltext search, custom metadata fields and attributes, WebDAV access for drag-and-drop file management from a desktop client, and integrations with third-party tools such as Collabora Online, OnlyOffice, Tesseract OCR, Apache Tika, Etherpad, HedgeDoc, and Paperless-ngx for document conversion and collaborative editing. The interface has been translated into more than 20 languages through community contributions, including English, German, French, Spanish, Italian, Dutch, Portuguese, Russian, Polish, and Czech, though translation completeness varies since the project relies on volunteer submissions rather than professional localization. SeedDMS is released under the GNU General Public License version 2 (GPL v2), which means the source code is free to use, modify, and redistribute, including for commercial hosting. Because it is entirely free and open source, SeedDMS has no pricing tiers, subscription plans, or paywalled features anyone can download it and use it for unlimited users and documents, but there is no official vendor-provided support, hosting, or SLA; users rely on the SourceForge project page, community forums, and documentation for help. This makes SeedDMS best suited for technically capable teams, IT departments, small and mid-sized businesses, and privacy-conscious organizations that want full control over where their documents live and are comfortable managing their own server setup, backups, and updates. It is a reasonable lower-cost alternative to commercial document management platforms for organizations with tighter budgets or strict data-residency requirements, though it lacks the polished onboarding, official support contracts, and turnkey enterprise integrations that paid platforms typically offer.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: SeedDMS is a free, open-source document management system written in PHP that organizations self-host to store, organize, and control access to their documents. It was forked from the LetoDMS project in February 2013 by developer Uwe Steinmann, who has led its development since, building on a lineage that traces back to the myDMS project started around 2002.

The software runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows and works with MySQL, SQLite3, or PostgreSQL, giving administrators flexibility in how and where they deploy it. As open-source software licensed under GPL v2, there is no cost to download, install, or run SeedDMS at any scale.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SeedDMS centers on document version control, so every edit is preserved and can be rolled back, paired with granular user and group permissions for controlling who can view, edit, or approve documents. A built-in workflow engine supports custom review and approval chains, which is useful for teams that need an audit trail on document sign-off.

Beyond the basics, SeedDMS offers fulltext search, custom metadata fields, WebDAV access for mounting the document store like a network drive, and integrations with tools like Collabora Online, OnlyOffice, Tesseract OCR, and Paperless-ngx for editing and text extraction directly from the DMS.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SeedDMS has no pricing plans because it is entirely free and open source under the GPL v2 license. There are no per-user fees, storage limits, or premium tiers to unlock additional features. The only real cost is the infrastructure and technical time required to self-host, secure, back up, and maintain the server it runs on, since there is no official paid support or hosting offering from the project.

features.title: Document versioning
features.description: Every change to a document is stored as a new version, with the ability to roll back to any previous revision.

features.title: Granular access control
features.description: Fine-grained permissions for individual users and groups control who can view, edit, approve, or delete documents and folders.

features.title: Configurable workflow engine
features.description: Custom review, approval, and revision workflows can be defined to route documents through sign-off chains.

features.title: Fulltext search
features.description: Documents and metadata can be searched across the entire repository to quickly locate files.

features.title: WebDAV access
features.description: Documents can be accessed and edited as if they were a mounted network drive using standard WebDAV clients.

features.title: Third-party integrations
features.description: Built-in support for Collabora Online, OnlyOffice, Tesseract OCR, Apache Tika, Etherpad, HedgeDoc, and Paperless-ngx for editing, OCR, and format conversion.

features.title: Custom metadata and attributes
features.description: Documents can carry author, keyword, description, and arbitrary custom attribute fields for organization and search.

features.title: Multi-language interface
features.description: The interface has been translated into more than 20 languages by community contributors.

pros: Completely free and open source with no per-user or per-document limits
Full control over data since it is entirely self-hosted
Mature codebase with over a decade of active development
Flexible database support (MySQL, SQLite3, PostgreSQL) and cross-platform compatibility
Extensible with useful third-party integrations like OCR and OnlyOffice
cons: Requires technical skill to install, secure, and maintain a self-hosted server
No official vendor support, hosting, or SLA is available
Interface looks dated compared to modern commercial SaaS document management tools
Community translations are inconsistent in completeness across languages
Development is led largely by a single maintainer, which can limit release pace
No native mobile app

faqs.question: Is SeedDMS free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. SeedDMS is free and open-source software released under the GPL v2 license, so there are no license fees for any number of users or documents.

faqs.question: Do I need to self-host SeedDMS?
faqs.answer: Yes. SeedDMS does not offer an official hosted or cloud version; it must be installed on your own server, VPS, or hosting environment.

faqs.question: What databases does SeedDMS support?
faqs.answer: SeedDMS supports MySQL, SQLite3, and PostgreSQL as its database backend.

faqs.question: Can SeedDMS do OCR on scanned documents?
faqs.answer: Yes, SeedDMS integrates with Tesseract OCR to extract text from scanned documents and images.

faqs.question: Who maintains SeedDMS?
faqs.answer: SeedDMS has been primarily maintained by developer Uwe Steinmann since he forked it from the LetoDMS project in February 2013.

faqs.question: Does SeedDMS support workflow approvals?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes a configurable workflow engine for document review, approval, and revision processes.

faqs.question: Is SeedDMS suitable for enterprise use?
faqs.answer: SeedDMS markets itself as enterprise-ready for small and mid-sized organizations, though it lacks some enterprise conveniences like official support contracts and built-in SSO.

faqs.question: What license is SeedDMS released under?
faqs.answer: SeedDMS is released under the GNU General Public License version 2 (GPL v2).

use_cases.title: Small business document storage
use_cases.description: A small business self-hosts SeedDMS to centralize contracts, invoices, and internal documentation with version history and access control, avoiding recurring SaaS subscription fees.
use_cases.audience: Small business owners and office administrators

use_cases.title: Compliance and audit trail workflows
use_cases.description: An operations or compliance team uses SeedDMS's workflow engine to route documents through review and approval steps with a full audit trail of changes.

use_cases.title: Privacy-conscious self-hosting
use_cases.description: A privacy-focused organization or IT department deploys SeedDMS on its own infrastructure to keep sensitive documents fully in-house rather than in a third-party cloud.

features: Full source code access under GPL v2
Community support via forums and documentation
No official vendor support or hosted option

enrichment.short_description: SeekOut is an AI-powered talent sourcing and recruiting platform that helps recruiters search over a billion candidate profiles, reach out with automated campaigns, and rediscover talent already in their ATS.
enrichment.pricing_model: Per-seat subscription with tiered and custom enterprise pricing
enrichment.starting_price: From $149 per month (billed annually) for the Recruit Core plan
enrichment.seo_title: SeekOut Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SeekOut is an AI recruiting and talent sourcing platform with access to 1B+ profiles. See 2026 pricing plans, key features, pros and cons, and top alternatives.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SeekOut is an AI-powered talent sourcing and recruiting platform founded in 2017 by Anoop Gupta (CEO) and Aravind Bala (CTO), headquartered in Bellevue, Washington. The company has raised roughly $189 million in venture funding and was valued at approximately $1.2 billion at its peak, and it says it is used by more than 750 enterprises for recruiting. SeekOut's core product lets recruiters search across more than one billion candidate profiles aggregated from public and professional sources, apply more than 30 search filters (including diversity and skills-based filters), and launch AI-driven multi-step outreach campaigns integrated with email. Its platform has expanded from a pure sourcing tool into what it calls an agentic AI recruiting platform, adding capabilities like ATS rediscovery (surfacing past candidates already sitting in a company's applicant tracking system), inbound resume evaluation and AI screening, market intelligence reporting, and a managed service called SeekOut Spot that provides on-demand AI sourcing or AI recruiting-agency-style services billed per role rather than per seat. Pricing is tiered: the entry-level Recruit Core plan starts at $149 per month when billed annually (or $179 per month billed monthly) and includes unlimited search, 500 monthly contact credits, 1,000 monthly exports, and AI campaign tools, aimed at solo recruiters or small teams. Higher tiers Sourcing, Sourcing plus Integration, and Full Recruiting Funnel are sold at custom per-seat pricing billed annually, adding features like ATS rediscovery, SSO, advanced diversity filters, dedicated customer success, and white-glove implementation as the tier increases; real-world negotiated enterprise contracts have been reported in the range of roughly $3,000 to $10,000 per seat per year depending on volume and features. Enterprise buyers typically pay anywhere from around $1,200 to $1,999 or more per month for broader team deployments, and larger multi-seat contracts require a custom quote from SeekOut's sales team. SeekOut competes directly with LinkedIn Recruiter, hireEZ, and Gem in the talent sourcing category, and it differentiates on the breadth of its candidate database, diversity-focused search filters, and increasingly on agentic AI workflows that automate parts of sourcing and initial screening. The company underwent notable workforce reductions in late 2023 and again in mid-2024 (cutting around 30 percent of staff in the second round) as part of a broader downturn in HR tech spending, which has since narrowed its headcount from earlier peaks even as the product itself continued to expand into new AI-driven capabilities.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: SeekOut was founded in 2017 by Anoop Gupta and Aravind Bala and is headquartered in Bellevue, Washington. It began as a talent sourcing search engine that indexes more than a billion candidate profiles and has since expanded into what the company brands an agentic AI recruiting platform, adding AI-driven outreach campaigns, ATS rediscovery, and AI screening on top of its original sourcing search.

The company has raised approximately $189 million in venture funding and reported a valuation near $1.2 billion, with more than 750 enterprise customers using the platform for recruiting as of recent company disclosures.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SeekOut's core strength is candidate search: recruiters can filter across more than 30 criteria, including skills, diversity attributes, and career signals, to find candidates who are not always visible on mainstream professional networks. AI-driven multi-step outreach campaigns and email integration let recruiters automate follow-ups with sourced candidates.

Newer capabilities include ATS rediscovery, which resurfaces qualified candidates already sitting in a company's applicant tracking system, plus inbound resume evaluation, AI screening, and market intelligence reporting for benchmarking talent pools and compensation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SeekOut Recruit Core starts at $149 per month billed annually ($179 billed monthly) and covers unlimited search, 500 monthly contact credits, and 1,000 monthly exports for a single seat, with a 14-day free trial. Higher tiers (Sourcing, Sourcing plus Integration, and Full Recruiting Funnel) use custom per-seat annual pricing that scales with contact credits, export limits, ATS integration, and support level, with enterprise deployments typically ranging from roughly $1,200 to $1,999 or more per month depending on team size.
SeekOut also offers SeekOut Spot, a managed recruiting service billed per search or per role with no long-term contract, positioned as a lower-cost alternative to traditional staffing agencies.

features.title: Talent search across 1B+ profiles
features.description: Search a database of more than a billion candidate profiles using over 30 filters, including skills, experience, and diversity attributes.

features.title: AI outreach campaigns
features.description: Launch automated, multi-step AI-generated email outreach sequences to sourced candidates directly from the platform.

features.title: ATS rediscovery
features.description: Surface qualified past candidates already stored in a company's applicant tracking system alongside new external candidates.

features.title: AI resume screening
features.description: Automatically evaluate and rank inbound applicants using AI-driven screening for the Full Recruiting Funnel tier.

features.title: Diversity and advanced filters
features.description: Search filters designed to help recruiters build more diverse candidate pipelines, available on higher-tier plans.

features.title: Market intelligence reporting
features.description: Benchmarking and analytics on talent pools, hiring trends, and market compensation data.

features.title: SSO and enterprise controls
features.description: Single sign-on and dedicated customer success support are available on Sourcing tier and above.

features.title: SeekOut Spot managed service
features.description: An on-demand, per-role AI sourcing or AI recruiting-agency service with no long-term contract required.

pros: Very large candidate database with strong search filtering, including diversity-focused filters
AI outreach campaigns save recruiters time on manual follow-up
ATS rediscovery helps surface overlooked candidates already in a company's own systems
Flexible entry point with a lower-cost single-seat plan for solo recruiters
Managed SeekOut Spot option for teams that want sourcing without hiring a full-time recruiter
cons: Higher-tier and enterprise pricing is not published and requires a custom sales quote
Per-seat and per-credit contact limits can restrict heavy outbound sourcing use
Company underwent significant workforce reductions in 2023 and 2024, raising questions about long-term investment pace
Real-world enterprise contract costs can run several thousand dollars per seat annually
Full functionality (ATS integration, AI screening) is reserved for the most expensive tiers

faqs.question: How much does SeekOut cost?
faqs.answer: SeekOut's entry-level Recruit Core plan starts at $149 per month billed annually ($179 billed monthly). Higher tiers use custom per-seat annual pricing that typically runs from roughly $1,200 to $1,999 or more per month for team deployments, depending on features and volume.

faqs.question: Does SeekOut offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, SeekOut offers a 14-day free trial on its Recruit Core plan.

faqs.question: When was SeekOut founded?
faqs.answer: SeekOut was founded in 2017 by Anoop Gupta and Aravind Bala.

faqs.question: Where is SeekOut headquartered?
faqs.answer: SeekOut is headquartered in Bellevue, Washington, United States.

faqs.question: What is SeekOut Spot?
faqs.answer: SeekOut Spot is a managed AI recruiting service billed per role or per search, with no long-term contract, positioned as a lower-cost alternative to traditional recruiting agencies.

faqs.question: Can SeekOut integrate with an ATS?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sourcing plus Integration and Full Recruiting Funnel tiers include ATS rediscovery and integration to search candidates already stored in a company's applicant tracking system.

faqs.question: How many candidate profiles does SeekOut search?
faqs.answer: SeekOut searches across more than one billion candidate profiles aggregated from public and professional sources.

faqs.question: Who are SeekOut's main competitors?
faqs.answer: SeekOut competes primarily with LinkedIn Recruiter, hireEZ, and Gem in the AI-powered talent sourcing category.

use_cases.title: Solo recruiter sourcing
use_cases.description: An independent recruiter or small team uses the Recruit Core plan to search candidate profiles and run AI outreach campaigns on a limited monthly budget.
use_cases.audience: Solo recruiters and small in-house talent teams

use_cases.description: A large enterprise talent acquisition team uses SeekOut's advanced diversity filters and market intelligence to build more representative candidate pipelines at scale.
